headline: ‘Fuck you, Bambu’: How one private message could change the face of 3D printing
summary: Bambu Lab, a leading 3D printer company, faces backlash after sending a private message. The company asked developer Paweł Jarczak to delete his open-source code. The 3D printing community is now rallying to fund a 'war' against Bambu.

headline: Meta lays off thousands of employees to offset AI investments
summary: Meta has laid off thousands of employees, citing efforts to run the company more efficiently. These job cuts are intended to compensate for the company's significant AI investments. Impacted staffers received notification via an email from Meta management.

headline: In SpaceX’s IPO, Elon Musk is a risk factor
summary: SpaceX's upcoming IPO reveals complex interactions and overlaps between Elon Musk's companies. The offering highlights how money is shuffled between his various ventures, making tracking difficult. Elon Musk's involvement is identified as a significant risk factor for the IPO.

headline: SpaceX just filed for what could be the biggest IPO ever
summary: SpaceX has formally filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC, commencing its IPO process. This could become the largest initial public offering ever, listing on Nasdaq with the ticker SPCX. The company reported $18.67 billion in revenue in 2025.

headline: The Boys Showrunner Explains Queen Maeve No-Show in Series Finale
summary: The Boys showrunner Eric Kripke wanted Queen Maeve to cameo in the series finale. Actress Dominique McElligott, who plays Maeve, turned down the role. Kripke provided an explanation for the character's absence from the final episode.

headline: Splitgate Dev Announces Titanfall-Inspired Shooter Empulse
summary: Splitgate developer 1047 Games has officially announced a new title named Empulse. The game is described as a 'Titanfall-inspired movement shooter.' Empulse is anticipated to enter early access later this year.

headline: Sony Quietly Increased the Price of PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium Subscriptions, Too
summary: Sony has quietly implemented price increases for PlayStation Plus Extra and Premium subscription tiers. Previously, only the Essential tier was publicly announced to receive a price hike. The new pricing now affects multiple levels of the PlayStation Plus service.

headline: Schwarzenegger Reveals King Conan Will Shoot in 2027
summary: Arnold Schwarzenegger announced that the long-awaited 'King Conan' movie will begin filming in 2027. This confirms a production timeline for the sequel to the classic fantasy films. Fans can anticipate the return of the iconic character in a few years.

headline: NHS trust sacks staff over attacks victims' records
summary: Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust has fired staff members for accessing records of attack victims. The Trust confirmed that investigations into these breaches are ongoing. This action addresses serious confidentiality violations within the healthcare system.

headline: Free bus trips for children in England this August as part of Reeves's cost-of-living plan
summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves plans to introduce free bus trips for children across England this August. This initiative is part of a series of announcements aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures. The measure seeks to provide relief for families during the summer.

headline: Chris Mason: Reeves' summer savings drive won't stretch to energy bills
summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves is set to announce new measures to help ease the cost of living this summer. However, her proposed savings drive will not include direct assistance with energy bills. The focus remains on other areas of household expenditure.

headline: EasyJet boss says summer flights won't be hit by jet fuel shortages
summary: EasyJet's chief executive assured that summer flights will not be impacted by jet fuel shortages. The airline has reported no issues with its fuel supply chain. Despite this, customer bookings are occurring later due to uncertainty from the Iran war.
